page: Amazon Web Services x2gd.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an ARM64-based AWS Graviton processor with 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs, running at 2.5 GHz. The CPU is based on the ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 architecture, equipped with 64 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. It includes hardware-accelerated AES cryptography but lacks AVX, AMX, and hyper-threading capabilities.

The instance is configured with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16.0 GB per core. For local storage, it includes a bundled 475 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume. Baseline network bandwidth is rated at 2 Gbps. This hardware profile is designed for memory-intensive workloads that benefit from local high-speed storage.

Performance benchmarks indicate average capabilities in multi-core processing and memory bandwidth, with stress-ng multi-core scoring 12111.79 and read-only small block memory bandwidth reaching 473.25 GB/sec. However, single-core performance is relatively weak, as shown by a Geekbench single-core score of 970.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 5259.32. Cryptography and compression tasks also rank in the lower percentiles, with a Passmark encryption score of 1094.0 and single-threaded gzip compression at 20.24 GB/sec.

Despite lower single-threaded performance, the instance demonstrates solid efficiency for database and memory-bound operations. It achieves 9.33 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and averages 5248.33 in Passmark database operations. The combination of high memory density, local NVMe storage, and energy-efficient Graviton ARM cores offers a cost-effective profile for memory-intensive applications, Redis caches, and database workloads, though it is poorly suited for heavy LLM inference or compute-intensive single-threaded tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services x2gd.2xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized instance powered by an 8-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.5 GHz. It features 128.0 GB of memory, yielding a high 16.0 GB per vCPU ratio, and includes a 475 GB local NVMe SSD instance store volume alongside a 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. While single-core performance and cryptographic speeds are relatively weak, as shown by a Geekbench single-core score of 970.0, the instance delivers solid multi-core and memory-bound performance. It achieves 9.33 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and shows stable database operation capabilities. This hardware configuration provides a cost-effective, high-density memory profile with local storage, making it well-suited for memory-intensive applications, caching layers, and database workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services x2gd.2xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized instance featuring 8 Graviton cores, 128 GB of RAM, and a 475 GB local NVMe SSD, optimized for database and caching workloads.

tagline: An ARM-powered, memory-optimized instance featuring high memory density and local NVMe storage for database and caching workloads.

128.0 GB of system memory providing 16.0 GB per core
Bundled 475 GB local NVMe SSD instance store volume
2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Strong performance in Redis SET operations and database benchmarks
Best suited for memory-intensive applications, caching, and databases
